title: lathyrus luteus subalpinus
intro: Lathyrus luteus subalpinus, also known as the Alpine Yellow Pea or Yellow Pea, is a subspecies of Lathyrus luteus, a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce oxidative stress. The health benefits associated with Lathyrus luteus subalpinus include its ability to support cardiovascular health, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus luteus subalpinus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various methods of consumption to harness its potential medicinal and health benefits.
